user_symptom: customers may have experienced difficulties connecting to resources. Impacted customers may have experienced timeouts or 401 authorization errors
root_cause: a pending (deployed, but awaiting reboot) OS security update which had introduced a networking change on the updated clusters

user_symptom: intermittent errors while accessing or performing service management operations - such as create, update, delete - for multiple resources from the Azure portal or when using CLI
root_cause: misconfiguration in the broad phase of a deployment for ARM services, which resulted in unanticipated utilization of a single partition of Cosmos DB

user_symptom: Issues attempting control plane operations (create, update, delete resources)
root_cause: A backend Cosmos DB was misconfigured to block legitimate access from Azure Resource Manager

user_symptom: service connection failures or possible timeouts
root_cause: A specific network traffic pattern combined with a networking stack configuration on the SQL Gateway instances triggered an imbalance on the CPU processing of new connection requests

user_symptom: Connections to Cosmos DB accounts in this region may have resulted in an error or timeout.
root_cause: Our frontend load balancing procedure had a regression that did not factor in the effect of a reduction in available cluster capacity, due to ongoing maintenance.

user_symptom: Impacted customers may have experienced timeouts or 401 authorization errors.
root_cause: This issue was triggered by a pending (deployed, but awaiting reboot) OS security update which had introduced a networking change on the updated clusters.

user_symptom: customers may have experienced issues accessing or using Azure, Microsoft 365 and Power Platform services
root_cause: A utility voltage sag was caused by a lightning strike on electrical infrastructure approximately 18 miles from the impacted Availability Zone

user_symptom: customers may have experienced issues with multiple Azure services... including service availability and connectivity issues, and service management failures
root_cause: an update to the allow list was being generated for publication to Storage scale units... published an allow list with incomplete VM Host address range information

service_name: Azure SQL Database and Data Warehouse
user_symptom: new connections to databases in these regions may have resulted in an error or timeout
root_cause: a recent maintenance activity did not complete successfully which in-turn caused the gateways to hold an incorrect certificate configuration

user_symptom: Connections to Azure Cosmos DB accounts in this region may have resulted in an error or timeout.
root_cause: A configuration change was applied to the Azure Load Balancer... resulted in intermittent network connectivity issues... higher-than-expected time spent in the Kernel, which resulted in spikes of high CPU utilization across the cluster.

user_symptom: Connectivity and service availability errors, new and existing connections resulted in errors or timeouts
root_cause: Service Fabric timer callback fired multiple times incorrectly, nodes were incorrectly reported as unavailable, cluster lost quorum and went down

user_symptom: Read and write requests failed for West US, increased latency observed
root_cause: A rare failure condition in the configuration store for one of the West US clusters caused the cluster's front-end service to crash due to a code regression

user_symptom: data for Azure Monitor Log Analytics and Microsoft Sentinel failed to ingest
root_cause: A code deployment for the Azure Container Apps service contained a misconfiguration that blocked the service from starting normally

user_symptom: customers experienced database unavailability and may have seen errors when connecting to their database instances
root_cause: A race condition in the underlying Remote Procedure Call (RPC) mechanism caused the network control plane channel in some cases to not pick up the rotated certificate, leading to connectivity failures once the certificate expired

user_symptom: database connectivity errors, issues performing service management operations, new connections may have failed
root_cause: memory hardware failure in a network router during automated maintenance

user_symptom: unresponsiveness and startup failures on Windows machines using the CrowdStrike Falcon agent
root_cause: CrowdStrike antivirus software update, which resulted in Windows OS virtual machines experiencing a bug check (BSOD) and entering a continuous restart loop

user_symptom: network congestion, dropping around 10% of traffic, and causing high latency for services traversing the network between data centers
root_cause: a software update on the SWAN network was unable to generate and program a working Forwarding Information Base (FIB) into the SWAN routers due to a bad configuration push

user_symptom: degraded performance, network drops, or timeouts when accessing Azure resources
root_cause: routing protocol metric change within an ISP backbone network

user_symptom: a subset of customers in the South Central US region may have experienced increased latency and/or intermittent connectivity issues while accessing services hosted in the region
root_cause: a single router in the RNG experienced a hardware fault, causing a fraction of network packets to be dropped

user_symptom: network connectivity issues, long network latency and/or timeouts when attempting to connect to resources hosted in Azure regions
root_cause: A network engineer performing an operational task to add network capacity to the global Wide Area Network (WAN) in Madrid, where a command to purge the IGP database was executed incorrectly

user_symptom: Network connections that originated from or were destined to West Europe (Amsterdam) or Southeast Asia (Singapore) regions may have resulted in a connection failure. Two-thirds of the new connection attempts would have been impacted
root_cause: The incident was caused by a configuration error pushed to routers in Microsoft Wide Area Network (WAN), in Singapore and Amsterdam. The configuration change sent all SYN packets (used to initiate a connection) to the router CPU for inspection, which caused the router's built-in denial of service protection to drop a subset of packets to protect the router CPU.

user_symptom: intermittent network failures and packet loss, lower transmission throughput, long delays in connections and connection failures
root_cause: One specific router in London intermittently started dropping network packets on one line card

user_symptom: a subset of customers experienced issues connecting to their services
root_cause: an incorrect IPv4 network prefix was applied during router configuration update

user_symptom: connectivity issues such as network drops, latency, and/or degradation when attempting to access or manage Azure resources
root_cause: Azure WAN began to experience a sudden and significant increase of traffic, upwards of 60 Tbps in additional traffic compared to the normal levels

service_name: Azure Front Door and Azure CDN Standard
user_symptom: intermittent HTTPS request connectivity failures when trying to reach their applications
root_cause: A code defect in the rolled-out version resulted in a race condition where a few servers in the impacted locations tried to revert to a full TLS handshake. This race condition resulted in these servers dropping HTTPS requests.

user_symptom: received error notifications when performing service management operations – such as create, delete, update, scale, start, stop
root_cause: one of the partitions associated with physical AZ-02 experienced data access issues because the underlying data store had exhausted an internal resource limit, and a resource creation policy configuration issue resulted in blocking all new VM creations in the AZ

user_symptom: Up to 50% of customer requests in the West Europe region failed, issues using Azure Resource Manager when performing resource management operations
root_cause: A positive feedback loop leading to saturation on the ARM web API tier, triggered by a new cache implementation that caused high-volume, short-held lock contention

user_symptom: Customers may have experienced error notifications when trying to access the Azure Portal and actions to load new HTTP content may have resulted in error notifications
root_cause: Layer 7 distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks causing traffic spikes overwhelming origin servers

user_symptom: intermittent network failures and packet loss while trying to connect to resources behind a Software Load Balancer
root_cause: A change in latest code deployment which caused some MUX instances to store the backend DIPs in a different order

user_symptom: delays in long running control plane operations including create, update and delete operations on resources, timeouts and operation failures
root_cause: During a certificate rotation, the processes tracking these background operations were repeatedly crashing due to a latent bug in the update process

user_symptom: a subset of customers using Virtual Machines (VMs) in the East US region experienced connectivity issues
root_cause: UPS rectifier failure on a Primary UPS and a previously discovered BIOS software bug

user_symptom: a subset of customers in West Central US may have experienced issues connecting to resources hosted in this region
root_cause: an ice storm in the region resulted in a loss of power to both primary and secondary utility power feeds... a subset of generators failed and UPS units supporting the downstream racks carried the load until their batteries drained

user_symptom: a subset of customers experienced difficulties connecting to resources hosted in one particular Availability Zone
root_cause: Unplanned power oscillation in datacenter causing Air Handling Units to shut down

user_symptom: increased latency or difficulties connecting to Azure services
root_cause: a multiple fiber-cut event isolated the Stockholm and Helsinki Azure Edge sites

user_symptom: intermittent connection errors, timeouts, or latency spikes while connecting to Microsoft services
root_cause: a network misconfiguration caused congestion and packet loss for AFD frontends

user_symptom: New connections to databases in this region may have resulted in errors or timeouts
root_cause: An operator error led to an incorrect action being performed in close sequence on all four persisted metadata caches

user_symptom: Connectivity issues, all connections may have failed for a subset of customers. Retries would have been unsuccessful.
root_cause: Due to a procedural error, the new connections were erroneously routed to a newly built gateway ring whose configuration was incomplete.

user_symptom: packet drops, timeouts, and/or increased latency
root_cause: fiber cut caused by severe weather conditions in the Netherlands

user_symptom: service degradation and increased latency for resources utilizing Azure Storage, including failure of virtual machine disks
root_cause: Incorrect limits set on the scale unit which allowed more load than desirable to be placed on it, and load balancing automation was not sufficiently spreading the load to other scale units

user_symptom: intermittent periods of degraded connectivity when attempting to connect to Azure, Microsoft 365, and Xbox resources
root_cause: Dual fiber path failure and router configuration issue causing site isolation

user_symptom: Customers may have had issues connecting to multiple Azure services and viewing or managing resources via the Azure Portal
root_cause: Misattribution of domain ownership and updating nameservers to inactive servers during an internal audit

user_symptom: Greater than expected throttling when performing requests against Storage resources, intermittent failures and degraded performance
root_cause: A new configuration was rolled out to improve the throttling algorithm, which had adverse effects on a small set of scale units, unexpectedly throttling some storage accounts

user_symptom: customers experienced failures when trying to access resources hosted in the West US 2 region
root_cause: utility power outage that affected all datacenters in the region, with failures in backup power systems in two datacenters

user_symptom: DNS resolution failures when connecting to an Azure Storage account, failures with launching clusters and serverless compute resources
root_cause: issue with DNS resolution for Azure Storage accounts

user_symptom: intermittent latency or issues connecting to resources hosted in West Europe
root_cause: Two separate events: local activity causing packet corruption and a significant fiber-cut

user_symptom: intermittent connection errors, timeouts, or increased latency while connecting to Microsoft services
root_cause: routine configuration change with an erroneous configuration file causing high memory allocation rates

user_symptom: Customers attempting to leverage Azure Resource Manager (ARM) may have experienced issues when performing resource management operations
root_cause: A latent code defect in ARM's Continuous Access Evaluation (CAE) preview feature caused ARM nodes to fail on startup when authentication to Entra failed

user_symptom: higher latency and packet loss for communications between services hosted in the Commercial cloud and the Azure China cloud
root_cause: an increase in traffic between the China Telecom network and the Azure Commercial cloud

service_name: Azure Front Door and Azure CDN
user_symptom: Increased latency, connection timeouts, and connection failures when connecting to Azure Front Door and Azure CDN
root_cause: Software regression in background service update causing CPU consumption spike to 40-60% of capacity

user_symptom: Customers experienced failures when attempting to provision new resources in a subset of Azure services
root_cause: A latent performance bug in the Registration Authority (RA), inadvertently introduced in the past month, caused requests to be throttled and unable to recover

user_symptom: increased latency, packet loss, failed connections and authentication failures across multiple Azure services
root_cause: A code defect in a version update of a component that controls network traffic routing between Azure regions

location: West Central US and Central US
user_symptom: Intermittent failures in Access Panel functionality including SSO, MFA registration, password reset, and group management
root_cause: Code change bug and auto-scaling configuration unable to handle traffic increase during failover

user_symptom: delays, timeouts, and failures which were visible for long running operations executed on the platform
root_cause: A code modification which started rolling out on 6 Jan 2022 exposed a latent defect in the infrastructure used to process long running operations

user_symptom: Customers may have encountered errors performing authentication operations for all Microsoft and third-party applications and services
root_cause: A service update targeting an internal validation test ring was deployed, causing a crash upon startup in the Azure AD backend services

user_symptom: service management errors, delays, and/or timeouts, GET and PUT errors, network connectivity issues
root_cause: Compute Resource Provider (CRP) Gateway service experienced an issue which severely reduced its throughput, retry storm triggered by the zonal failure of a related Allocator service

user_symptom: customers may have received failure notifications or 404 errors when attempting to create new resources through the Azure Portal
root_cause: a code change that was introduced which caused a key extension to fail, causing a null-reference within the Marketplace blade

user_symptom: intermittent errors when attempting to access the Azure portal and other Microsoft and Azure services
root_cause: code regression in a recent deployment to our edge DNS servers which introduced lock contention issues

user_symptom: intermittent connection failures when trying to access some virtual machines and virtual machines may have also restarted unexpectedly
root_cause: an ongoing OS update deployment task inadvertently contained a code configuration error that resulted in a number of previously addressed bug fixes being reverted on a subset of clusters

user_symptom: Customers may have received the following error message 'AADSTS90033: A Transient error has occurred. Please try again.'
root_cause: An unexpected increase in traffic volume and resource utilization of infrastructure in the region responsible for acquiring authentication tokens resulted in regional contention which exceeded operational thresholds

user_symptom: a subset of customers experienced errors when using Azure Resource Manager to perform service management operations
root_cause: A synchronization issue occurred between backend components used to permit certain ARM requests. A configuration change was applied to these backend components, which resulted in some instances of the ARM service becoming unreachable

user_symptom: a subset of customers using Azure Storage Premium File Shares in East US may have experienced issues accessing services
root_cause: a feature was applied to optimize the performance of IO operations... contained a code bug in an infrequent error path

user_symptom: failures while attempting to authenticate, attempts may have succeeded on retry
root_cause: an underlying code defect triggered some service nodes to experience a shortage of outbound ports to complete network tasks

user_symptom: intermittent errors when attempting to access the Azure portal and other Microsoft and Azure services
root_cause: configuration to connect to a required backend storage started failing after a maintenance to update their configuration was deployed

user_symptom: sign-in failures for a subset of Microsoft Accounts and sign-in issues when attempting to access Azure, Dynamics 365, and/or Microsoft 365 Services
root_cause: A regular operating system (OS) update was deployed to endpoints of a backend service in the Azure AD authentication stack, which interacted with the service in an unexpected way, making the service on the updated endpoints unresponsive

user_symptom: customers may have received failure notifications when performing service management operations - such as start, create, update, delete
root_cause: tooling provided an ARM template for use in performing these migrations... marked the Windows VM Agent extension as visible to the publishing subscription only in the ARM regional service after migration

user_symptom: Service management operations including create, rename, update and delete may have been impacted. Connectivity to database resources was not impacted.
root_cause: A manual maintenance operation impacted instances of an internal cluster data service that is responsible for receiving and executing service management operations. The primary instance of the data service became unhealthy preventing some of the service management operations from completing.

user_symptom: intermittently received errors when performing service management operations
root_cause: The request queue limit on some frontends for the disk management reached their limits and were rejecting a subset of requests

user_symptom: failure notifications when performing service management operations, errors when running pipelines and job executions for dataflow activities, errors when attempting to provision SQL Server Integration Services (SSIS) and Integration Runtime
root_cause: A new capacity configuration was deployed during a scale-up operation, triggering a conflict with an existing backend service configuration and causing an increase in the failure rate of service discovery requests

user_symptom: customers may have encountered failures when performing service management operations and authentication failures when attempting to access the Azure portal or other Azure resources
root_cause: OCSP and CRL endpoints were not reachable from clients in China due to a telecom provider issue

user_symptom: some customers experienced failures when requesting managed identity tokens or performing management operations
root_cause: key rollover operation that was not correctly deployed due to regions being pinned to an older build

user_symptom: Customers may have received an HTTP status code 502 (Bad Gateway) or HTTP status code 504 (Gateway Timeout)
root_cause: a configuration change was compounded by a surge in traffic which exceeded the regions' operational thresholds

user_symptom: end-users of customers using Azure Active Directory B2C may have experienced errors and timeouts when attempting to sign in or sign up
root_cause: The service experienced a significant increase in workload in the affected region during a planned maintenance operation

service_name: Azure App Service and Azure Functions
user_symptom: Issues with deployments, WebJobs, SSH console, Diagnostic Tools, and SCM endpoint access
root_cause: Misconfiguration during domain name resolution zone configuration for SCM endpoints

user_symptom: Clients intermittently received HTTP (5XX) errors or connection failures
root_cause: Improperly encoded configuration file deployed during maintenance, with a bug in deployment pipeline causing incorrect service health notifications

user_symptom: Azure offerings experienced provisioning failures globally
root_cause: Backend service became unhealthy due to unexpected traffic spike during scheduled hardware maintenance

user_symptom: connection failures when trying to access some resources hosted in the region
root_cause: a subset of storage resources experienced a drop in network connectivity

user_symptom: HTTP 503 error codes when attempting to access the Azure Portal
root_cause: An internal automation workflow was generating a high volume of requests to a backend CosmosDB partition on which the Azure Portal relies

user_symptom: a subset of customers may have experienced issues accessing resources in their Azure Portal and received an 'Access Denied' error message
root_cause: a recent deployment task introduced a software bug which impacted the synchronization of Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) information

user_symptom: issues, timeouts, or failures for some service management operations for services leveraging Azure Resource Manager (ARM)
root_cause: a change to backend role instances leveraged by Azure Resource Manager

user_symptom: customers in these areas may have experienced intermittent issues connecting to Azure resources
root_cause: A regional networking fiber cut resulted in a brief loss of connectivity to Microsoft resources

user_symptom: intermittent 500-level errors or an intermittent latency when accessing the Azure portal
root_cause: The Azure portal frontend endpoints in the US North Central region experienced an increase in CPU usage

user_symptom: a subset of customers may have experienced traffic routing to unhealthy backends
root_cause: A configuration change was deployed, causing the incorrect routing of traffic to unhealthy backends

user_symptom: intermittent 400-level errors when attempting to access Microsoft Graph APIs
root_cause: a recent update to improve the underlying Microsoft Graph API infrastructure created a configuration issue between the Microsoft Graph API interface and its underlying Internet Information Services driver

user_symptom: difficulties connecting to the Azure Portal
root_cause: Under investigation

location: US Gov Texas and US Gov Arizona
user_symptom: intermittent issues or latency connecting to resources
root_cause: a recent maintenance event on network infrastructure in the US Gov Texas region led to a shift in network traffic, causing a single network device to become congested

user_symptom: customers using Azure DevOps in multiple regions may have observed connectivity errors to DevOps services
root_cause: an inadvertent error with a configuration change in the back-end service

user_symptom: performance issues and timeout errors
root_cause: scaling out process was affected by an internal infrastructure issue leading to high CPU usage

user_symptom: Timeouts and issues connecting to resources
root_cause: A backend network device became unhealthy, and traffic was not automatically rerouted

user_symptom: customers may have received failure notifications when performing service management operations—such as create/scale, update, and delete
root_cause: a recent deployment task impacted service-to-service communication which resulted in failure of management requests

user_symptom: customers may have experienced issues while trying to create an application on the Azure portal when signed-in with their Microsoft Account (MSA)
root_cause: insufficient capacity to handle requests

user_symptom: a subset of customers using Azure CDN from Verizon may have experienced service degradation
root_cause: a recent deployment task impacted connectivity to origins, causing dynamic or cache miss requests to fail

user_symptom: intermittent HTTP 500 errors or general latency when trying to access the Azure Portal
root_cause: the scaling process kicked in as expected but instances didn't serve traffic as fast as expected

user_symptom: degraded performance, network drops, or timeouts
root_cause: a network device, affecting network traffic in Argentina, experienced a hardware fault and that network traffic was not automatically rerouted

user_symptom: a limited subset of customers in the United Kingdom may have experienced connectivity issues to Azure sites and services
root_cause: a power event at a regional internet service provider network exchange

user_symptom: Failures with online transcription, batch transcription, custom speech, and translation
root_cause: During recent deployment, a part of the code lost access to KeyVault, preventing the App Service from running as expected
